name: gold-fresh-110-2026-06-05
description: Fresh generality suite — new analyst phrasing across all shapes + all tables + non-data routes (chat/knowledge/tool). Gold executed-validated.

Scenarios.Question: Roughly how many tickets are sitting in the system right now that nobody has soft-deleted?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0;

Scenarios.Question: Could you pull up every work order whose status currently reads Completed?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT * FROM WorkOrders WHERE Status = 'Completed';

Scenarios.Question: What does it come to if we add up the TotalAmount across all bills that have already been paid?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT SUM(TotalAmount) FROM Bills WHERE Status = 'Paid';

Scenarios.Question: On average, how long do our outages drag on in seconds from start to finish once they've ended?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT AVG(DATEDIFF(SECOND, StartedAt, EndedAt)) FROM Outages WHERE EndedAt IS NOT NULL;

Scenarios.Question: Break down the outage count for me by how severe each one was.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT Severity, COUNT(*) AS OutageCount FROM Outages GROUP BY Severity;

Scenarios.Question: Which payment cleared for the single largest amount we've ever received?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT MAX(Amount) FROM Payments;

Scenarios.Question: I need the tally of tickets that were opened anytime during the 2025 calendar year.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tickets WHERE CreatedAt >= '2025-01-01' AND CreatedAt < '2026-01-01';

Scenarios.Question: Show me the bills that have slipped past their due date and are now flagged Overdue.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT * FROM Bills WHERE Status = 'Overdue';

Scenarios.Question: Tell me how the work orders split out across the different OrderType values, with a head count for each.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT OrderType, COUNT(*) AS OrderCount FROM WorkOrders GROUP BY OrderType;

Scenarios.Question: Across every meter reading we've logged, what's the typical consumption figure?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT AVG(Consumption) FROM MeterReadings;

Scenarios.Question: What's the lowest CSAT score anyone has ever given us?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT MIN(Score) FROM CsatResponses;

Scenarios.Question: How many phone calls did the team handle over the course of March 2025?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM CallLogs WHERE StartedAt >= '2025-03-01' AND StartedAt < '2025-04-01';

Scenarios.Question: For each payment status, sum up the total amount that's been processed under it.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT Status, SUM(Amount) AS TotalAmount FROM Payments GROUP BY Status;

Scenarios.Question: Add together the affected-customer counts from all the outages on record so I can see the total reach.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT SUM(AffectedCustomerCount) FROM Outages;

Scenarios.Question: How many work orders are stuck in the OnHold state at the moment?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM WorkOrders WHERE Status = 'OnHold';

Scenarios.Question: Across the whole customer base, what's our headcount of registered customers right now?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS CustomerCount FROM Customers;

Scenarios.Question: Could you spell out the English names of every customer segment we maintain?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT NameEn FROM CustomerSegments;

Scenarios.Question: Which of our customer segments are flagged as eligible for subsidies? Show their names.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT NameEn FROM CustomerSegments WHERE IsSubsidyEligible = 1;

Scenarios.Question: How big is our service-account portfolio in terms of total accounts on file?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS ServiceAccountCount FROM ServiceAccounts;

Scenarios.Question: I need the account numbers for every service account whose status reads Active.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT AccountNumber FROM ServiceAccounts WHERE Status = 'Active';

Scenarios.Question: Run me a tally of the technicians currently on staff.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS TechnicianCount FROM Technicians WHERE IsActive = 1;

Scenarios.Question: Pull the EmployeeCode and English name of any technician whose specialty is Electrical.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT EmployeeCode, FullNameEn FROM Technicians WHERE Specialty = 'Electrical';

Scenarios.Question: Enumerate the English labels of all the departments we operate.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT NameEn FROM Departments;

Scenarios.Question: Look up the region named Damascus and tell me what RegionType it's classified as.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT RegionType FROM Regions WHERE NameEn = 'Damascus';

Scenarios.Question: Among our service types, which ones are still switched on? Give me their English names and measurement units.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT NameEn, Unit FROM ServiceTypes WHERE IsActive = 1;

Scenarios.Question: Show me the payment methods customers can pay digitally with, listed by name.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT NameEn FROM PaymentMethods WHERE IsDigital = 1;

Scenarios.Question: Just tell me the count of distinct service points sitting in our network.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS ServicePointCount FROM ServicePoints;

Scenarios.Question: Give me the asset codes and English names for assets that are of type Transformer.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT AssetCode, NameEn FROM Assets WHERE AssetType = 'Transformer';

Scenarios.Question: I need two figures on our work orders: first, how many of them have actually been wrapped up, and second, what's the typical turnaround in hours from creation to completion across those finished ones?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM WorkOrders WHERE CompletedAt IS NOT NULL;

Scenarios.Question: Break the outages down by the region they hit and tell me the headcount of customers knocked out in each — then on top of that, point me to the single outage that affected the most customers.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT r.NameEn AS RegionName, SUM(o.AffectedCustomerCount) AS AffectedCustomers FROM Outages o JOIN Regions r ON o.RegionId = r.Id GROUP BY r.NameEn ORDER BY AffectedCustomers DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Could you let me know how many tickets are sitting unresolved right now, and alongside that what fraction of the entire ticket pile that represents?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NULL AND IsDeleted = 0;

Scenarios.Question: Walk me through payments grouped by how customers paid — give the running total of money taken per method — and also surface which payment method brought in the largest sum.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT pm.NameEn AS PaymentMethod, SUM(p.Amount) AS TotalCollected FROM Payments p JOIN PaymentMethods pm ON p.PaymentMethodId = pm.Id GROUP BY pm.NameEn ORDER BY TotalCollected DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Two things about the bills, please: what's the grand total amount that's been billed out, and what does an individual bill average out to?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT SUM(TotalAmount) AS GrandTotal, AVG(TotalAmount) AS AveragePerBill FROM Bills;

Scenarios.Question: Tally up the maintenance schedules per region for me, and while you're at it tell me how many regions ended up with no maintenance scheduled at all.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT r.NameEn AS RegionName, COUNT(m.Id) AS ScheduleCount FROM Regions r LEFT JOIN MaintenanceSchedules m ON m.RegionId = r.Id GROUP BY r.NameEn;

Scenarios.Question: Show me the spread of tickets across regions — I want each region's name next to its ticket count.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT r.NameEn AS RegionName, COUNT(t.Id) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN Regions r ON t.RegionId = r.Id WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY r.NameEn ORDER BY TicketCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Which five regions are carrying the heaviest outage load? Rank them by how many outages each has seen and cap it at the top five.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 5 r.NameEn AS RegionName, COUNT(o.Id) AS OutageCount FROM Outages o JOIN Regions r ON o.RegionId = r.Id GROUP BY r.NameEn ORDER BY OutageCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: I'm after the customer segments that are pulling their weight — list any segment with at least 50 service accounts tied to it, along with that account tally.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT cs.NameEn AS SegmentName, COUNT(sa.Id) AS AccountCount FROM ServiceAccounts sa JOIN CustomerSegments cs ON sa.CustomerSegmentId = cs.Id GROUP BY cs.NameEn HAVING COUNT(sa.Id) >= 50 ORDER BY AccountCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Are there any regions that have stayed completely outage-free? Hand me the names of regions with not a single outage on record.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT r.NameEn AS RegionName FROM Regions r LEFT JOIN Outages o ON o.RegionId = r.Id WHERE o.Id IS NULL;

Scenarios.Question: Find me the customers who've never put a single payment through — I just want the people with zero payments to their name.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT c.Id, c.FullNameEn FROM Customers c LEFT JOIN Bills b ON b.CustomerId = c.Id LEFT JOIN Payments p ON p.BillId = b.Id WHERE p.Id IS NULL GROUP BY c.Id, c.FullNameEn;

Scenarios.Question: For the bills issued during 2025, how many fall under each service type? Give me the service type's name and its bill count.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT st.NameEn AS ServiceType, COUNT(b.Id) AS BillCount FROM Bills b JOIN ServiceTypes st ON b.ServiceTypeId = st.Id WHERE b.IssuedAt >= '2025-01-01' AND b.IssuedAt < '2026-01-01' GROUP BY st.NameEn ORDER BY BillCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Per region, how many work orders have been raised? Pair each region name with its work-order count.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT r.NameEn AS RegionName, COUNT(w.Id) AS WorkOrderCount FROM WorkOrders w JOIN Regions r ON w.RegionId = r.Id GROUP BY r.NameEn ORDER BY WorkOrderCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Tell me which complaint types are genuinely common — only show a type if more than 20 tickets have been filed under it, and include that ticket count next to the type name.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT ct.NameEn AS ComplaintType, COUNT(t.Id) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN ComplaintTypes ct ON t.ComplaintTypeId = ct.Id WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY ct.NameEn HAVING COUNT(t.Id) > 20 ORDER BY TicketCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Busy day on your end so far?
Does all this number-crunching ever wear you out?
Roughly how many dollars does one British pound fetch right now?
Is there a public holiday on the horizon over in Germany?
When a payment gets recorded, what details do we actually capture on it?
Help me get my head around it — how is a service account different from a service point in practice?
What set of severity levels is an outage allowed to be flagged with?
Remind me — what kinds of things are you here to help me with?
